deferred_reason: Same as row 07: no existing BDS cluster in oase tenancy + user chose to skip provisioning. The skill `aidp-bds-hive` Option A (LDAP) documents the canonical pattern (`auth=ldap`, basic user/password JDBC); live validation is contingent on a customer-provided BDS cluster with HiveServer2 LDAP auth enabled.

discovery_summary.additional_blocker_for_ldap: Even if a BDS Standard cluster were provisioned, BDS does not ship with an LDAP server. HiveServer2 LDAP auth requires either an external LDAP/AD server or a stand-up of OpenLDAP on a separate Compute VM (which would extend the provisioning surface significantly).

skill_correctness: The aidp-bds-hive SKILL.md LDAP snippet matches the official AIDP `aidataplatform` HIVE format pattern from oracle-samples/oracle-aidp-samples (`Read_Write_External_Ecosystem_Connectors.ipynb`): `authentication.method=basic` with user.name/password. Direct Hive JDBC also supported via `auth=ldap` URL fragment. Both forms are documented.
to_unblock: Provide a customer BDS cluster with HiveServer2 LDAP auth enabled. Need: HS2 hostname, port (usually 10000), an LDAP-bind user with Hive privileges, password. Then run `examples/bds_hive_ldap.ipynb` against it.
